title: 4-Bit Binary Adder-Subtractor using 2's Complement
description: A logic circuit diagram showing how a single hardware unit can perform both addition and subtraction of two 4-bit binary numbers (A and B) by using a control line and XOR gates to conditionally invert the subtrahend.
relationships: The Mode Control line is connected to the second input of all four XOR gates. Input B bits (B0-B3) are fed into the first input of their respective XOR gates. The output of each XOR gate is fed into one input of its corresponding Full Adder. Input A bits (A0-A3) are fed directly into the second input of their Full Adders. The Carry-Out (Cout) of each Full Adder is chained to the Carry-In (Cin) of the next more significant bit. The initial Carry-In (C0) is connected directly to the Mode Control line. The sum outputs (S0-S3) of the Full Adders form the result. The final Carry-Out (C4) and the most significant bits are used for overflow detection.
